other_observers: Brenda Linfield Doug Faulkner Doug's wife; I was not the original finder of this bird. Its presence had previously been reported on cobirds.
description: It was feeding on the ground with many Canada Geese.  It was at the small end of the size range in the flock of Canada Geese.  Its back was as dark as the darkest Canada Goose, or perhaps a bit darker. There were prominent white feather edgings on the wings, near where they met the sides of the breast (upper flanks).  The bill was dark, with a paler section near the base (this could have been on the face).  The entire head and neck, down to the upper breast, was solid black, except for a dingy white collar. This white collar was 1" - 2" wide.  The lower breast was blackish, paler than the neck.Behaviors: feeding on the ground with many Canada GeeseCall: none heardPlumage: adult
